summary: Deliver C3 as a safe two-dimensional drawing viewer: fail-closed engine-SVG isolation, exact engine-summary dimensions, cursor-centered pan and zoom, automatic 2D/3D routing, and conservatively disclosed pinned-mode mismatch behavior while retaining the complete C2 3D surface.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, decisions, and unit of work
Section 7 V-1, V-2, V-3, and V-6

decisions.decision: Parse engine output as SVG, reject malformed, non-SVG, oversized, active, linked, styled, or network-capable content, permit only the pinned exporter's small element and attribute vocabulary, and display only a sanitized data URL rather than inserting engine markup into the application DOM.
decisions.rationale: Engine text crosses an untrusted document boundary. A fixed allowlist retains the official exporter's geometry while failing closed against active content and external resource access.

decisions.decision: Use the pinned engine's geometry-summary bounding box for dimensions and normalize sanitized SVG width, height, and viewBox to those bounds instead of treating the exporter's presentation-margin viewBox as geometry.
decisions.rationale: OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 adds presentation margins. The geometry summary reports exact model bounds and therefore supports truthful dimensions and stable fitting.

decisions.decision: Represent the viewport as a model-space center and positive millimeters-per-pixel scale; preserve the model point beneath the cursor during wheel zoom, use pointer drag and arrow keys for pan, plus/minus for centered zoom, and zero or the Fit control to refit.
decisions.rationale: A single affine state makes cursor anchoring and scale disclosure deterministic and testable while retaining keyboard operation.

decisions.decision: Route successful two- and three-dimensional results by their engine discriminator. If a user pin conflicts with the current result, show no incompatible geometry and display an explicit mismatch notice until automatic mode or a compatible result is selected.
decisions.rationale: Q-0024 leaves the owner-final mismatch presentation open. The conservative empty state neither overrides user intent nor presents stale or incompatible geometry as current.

testEvidence.criterion: C3 safe SVG, exact bounds, and viewport behavior
testEvidence.test: The sanitizer strips the external doctype, normalizes exact model bounds, rejects active and malformed content, viewport math preserves cursor anchoring, and the component exposes dimensions, scale, fit, zoom, and fail-closed UI.

red.outcome: RED: 3 test files failed and all 14 tests failed on behavior assertions against inert, type-correct implementations; failures covered unsafe SVG acceptance, unnormalized bounds, incorrect viewport math, absent controls, and absent fail-closed presentation.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 test files passed; 14 tests passed; duration 939 ms.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-3.a successful 2D results reach the document viewer
testEvidence.test: A native-service RenderSuccess2D is retained as the active document's last-good presentation rather than being discarded by the C2-only 3D branch.

red.command: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "presents a successful two-dimensional render"
red.outcome: RED: 1 test failed, 17 were filtered; the expected 2D viewer presentation was undefined.

green.command: node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "presents a successful two-dimensional render"
green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test passed, 17 were filtered; duration 661 ms.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-3.2 automatic and pinned routing without C2 regressions
testEvidence.test: Viewer state isolates per-document mode, active presentation carries 2D success, the pane switches 2D to 3D by discriminator, incompatible pins disclose an empty state, and the C2 viewer controls remain intact.

red.command: No complete transcript survived the capacity interruption after the first mode and presentation changes.
red.outcome: The initial agent reported staging assertion-level mode and 2D-presentation REDs, but its turn ended at model capacity before it returned exact counts. This ledger does not invent them.

green.outcome: GREEN: 8 test files passed; 52 tests passed; duration 1.93 s.

testEvidence.test: Render literal square([30,20]); through exact OpenSCAD 2026.06.12, compare the engine summary with an independent SVG-path parse, and verify the retained artifact inventory.

red.command: Retained verifier falsification changed the copied SVG width to 31 mm.
red.outcome: RED: the verifier returned FAIL with exit 1 for the tampered artifact; the temporary copy was removed.

green.outcome: GREEN: PASS. Executable SHA-256 DE9A0C732C23C3FEB0B49CF938777AA0AEE3E206DB9E98571672CACC4816C524; input SHA-256 358FDFCA08D4BC88B4A12641BF440346B8BA7B1FF2176375A43555CB6D19CBEF; SVG SHA-256 E6F944FDD2FABDE08D29BD5F0D3ACE8A8C016BDF4C5B45230FAF86796F09B03C; reported and independently parsed size 30 by 20 mm within 0.000001 mm; 27 artifact files inventoried.

testEvidence.criterion: Widened C3 regression, browser, native, and policy evidence
testEvidence.test: Run all TypeScript tests, Chromium E2E, typecheck, lint/source policy, build, generated parser, npm licenses, and exact-pinned native acceptance; reproduce the known Rust policy result without changing it.

red.outcome: KNOWN POLICY RED: exactly 19 packages use Unicode-3.0 or an expression containing it; Q-0001 remains the only reported Rust license-policy block.

green.command: pnpm.cmd test; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build; pnpm.cmd check:generated; node scripts/check-licenses.mjs npm; pnpm.cmd exec playwright test --workers=1 --retries=0; SCADMILL_OPENSCAD=<OpenSCAD-2026.06.12> cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/native-engine/Cargo.toml
green.outcome: GREEN: Vitest 95 files and 671 tests; Playwright 8 of 8; typecheck passed; lint checked 249 files and source policy passed; Vite built 165 modules; generated parser was clean; npm licenses passed for 117 packages; exact-pinned native acceptance passed 39 tests.

questions: Q-0001 remains open: Rust license policy rejects exactly 19 Unicode-3.0 records; C3 adds no dependency and does not alter the policy.
Q-0024 remains open: incompatible pinned-mode presentation uses the conservative empty pane and explicit mismatch notice pending the owner's final choice.

The retained AC-3.b artifact proves exact pinned-engine SVG and bounds output; component tests separately prove product readout and routing. They are not described as one browser-level engine-to-pane execution.

The first widened typecheck found only a missing subscribeOutput member in the new runtime test double. The fixture was corrected, production behavior was unchanged, and the full typecheck was rerun green.

No dedicated real-Chromium C3 interaction fixture exists at this stack point; happy-dom covers routing, sanitization, pan, zoom, fit, dimensions, and mismatch while the full eight-test Chromium suite guards adjacent UI behavior.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
